text:
Overview
Microsoft Sentinel is a scalable, cloud-native SIEM and SOAR solution that ingests
telemetry from Microsoft and third-party sources into a Log Analytics workspace, applies
analytics rules and UEBA/ML detections, and enables automated response through
Playbooks (Logic Apps). It underpins most modern Microsoft-centric SOC operations.
Core Architecture Components
Component
Role
Log Analytics
Workspace
Backing data store; KQL query engine over ingested tables
Data Connectors
Native connectors (Defender suite, Entra ID, AWS, GCP, syslog/CEF)
or Codeless Connector Platform
Analytics Rules
Scheduled/near-real-time/ML rules that generate Incidents from
suspicious patterns
Workbooks
Interactive dashboards for hunting and reporting
Playbooks
Logic Apps that automate investigation/response (isolate host,
disable user, notify)
Watchlists
Curated reference lists (VIP users, known-bad IPs) used to enrich/
filter detections
Content Hub /
Solutions

disable user, notify)
Watchlists
Curated reference lists (VIP users, known-bad IPs) used to enrich/
filter detections
Content Hub /
Solutions
Packaged analytics rules, workbooks, connectors per product (e.g.,
"Microsoft Defender for Cloud")
Analytics Rule Types
Scheduled — KQL query run on an interval, most common custom detection type
NRT (Near Real Time) — sub-minute latency for time-critical detections
C L O U D C H E AT S H E E T · S E C U R I T Y
Microsoft Sentinel
Cloud-native SIEM + SOAR built on Azure Log Analytics for detection, investigation, and
response
• 
• 
Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 1 of 3

Microsoft Security — auto-imports high-fidelity alerts from Defender products as
Incidents
Fusion — ML-based multi-stage attack correlation across signal sources
ML Behavior Analytics — built-in UEBA anomaly detection for identity/entity behavior
Investigation Workflow
Incident (grouped alerts + entities) → Investigation graph (pivots across related entities: user, host, IP,
file) → Entity behavior timeline → Hunting queries (KQL) to validate scope → Playbook-driven
containment → Incident closed with classification (True/False Positive, reason).
KQL Essentials
Operator
Use
where
Filter rows by condition
summarize
Aggregate (count, dcount, arg_max) grouped by field
join
Correlate across tables (e.g., SigninLogs with AuditLogs)
bin()
Time-bucket for trend/anomaly analysis
extend
Add computed columns
project
Select/rename output columns
Cost & Ingestion Management
Pricing driven primarily by GB ingested/day — commitment tiers reduce per-GB cost at
scale

extend
Add computed columns
project
Select/rename output columns
Cost & Ingestion Management
Pricing driven primarily by GB ingested/day — commitment tiers reduce per-GB cost at
scale
Basic Logs / Auxiliary Logs tier for high-volume, low-query-frequency data (cheaper
ingestion, limited KQL)
Data Collection Rules (DCR) filter/transform data before ingestion to reduce noise
and cost
Set table-level retention independently from workspace default to control storage
cost

Pitfall
Fix

Page 2 of 3

Ingesting all logs at
verbose level by
default
Use DCR filtering/transformation and Basic Logs tier for high-volume,
low-value tables
Analytics rules with
no entity mapping
Map entities (account, host, IP) so incidents correlate and drive
UEBA/investigation graphs
Alert fatigue from
unfused, low-fidelity
rules
Tune thresholds, use Fusion, and suppress known-benign patterns via
watchlists
Playbooks with no
error handling
Add try/catch branches in Logic Apps; alert if automated response
itself fails
No retention
strategy per table
Set table-level retention distinct from workspace default to balance
cost and investigation needs
Interview / Exam Quick Hits
Sentinel is Log Analytics + security content layer, not a separate data store
Fusion detections correlate low-fidelity signals into a single high-confidence
multistage incident
Playbooks are triggered manually, automatically on incident creation, or on a schedule

Fusion detections correlate low-fidelity signals into a single high-confidence
multistage incident
Playbooks are triggered manually, automatically on incident creation, or on a schedule
UEBA baselines normal behavior per entity over ~time window to flag deviations
(impossible travel, anomalous sign-in)
MITRE ATT&CK mapping is built into analytics rules for coverage-gap analysis via the
MITRE workbook
